infrastructure.secret_management: env vars + Redis transient + AES-256-GCM for persisted channel tokens

findings.title: Production Dockerfiles missing USER directive
findings.description: Four production containers run their CMD as root. No user: override in docker-compose.yml or docker-compose.prod.yml. Only docker/base-image/Dockerfile (agent base) drops to USER developer.
findings.exploit_scenario: Any RCE in the FastAPI backend would land as root inside the backend container. Backend has /var/run/docker.sock:ro mounted, so an attacker with read access can enumerate every Trinity agent container and read labels. Read-only socket blocks docker exec/run, but escalates the impact of any future write primitive (volume writes, Redis ops via platform network).
findings.impact: Defense-in-depth gap. Not a standalone vulnerability — requires a separate RCE — but widens blast radius of any future RCE finding.
findings.recommendation: Add a non-root user near the end of each production Dockerfile, e.g. `RUN groupadd --gid 1000 trinity && useradd --uid 1000 --gid trinity --home /app trinity && chown -R trinity:trinity /app` then `USER trinity`. Pattern already exists in docker/base-image/Dockerfile:70-132.

rejected_candidates.reason: GitHub Actions auto-masks ${{ secrets.* }} values in logs; default fallback CiTestPassword!1 is a documented public CI placeholder used only on forks lacking org secrets.
Documented test-only placeholder for schema-parity test on empty in-memory SQLite DB. Migration path never invokes encrypt(). Hits FP rule: 'test fixtures not imported by non-test code'.
